text: Describes the founding objects of SIIF and the services offered by the Incubation Cell including infrastructure, funding, mentorship, and events.

metadata.topic: Sathak Innovation and Incubation Foundation (SIIF) - Overview

text: To promote commerce, science, education and research by helping, establishing and incubating startups, individuals, teams with innovative ideas to make prototypes and convert them into products.

metadata.topic: Sathak Innovation and Incubation Foundation (SIIF) - Main Objectives

text: To promote a culture of innovation-driven entrepreneurship in the fields of science, engineering, technology, commerce, agriculture, health care, medicine, pharmacy, education, arts, sports and other various fields.

metadata.topic: Sathak Innovation and Incubation Foundation (SIIF) - Main Objectives

text: To establish, help establish and/or manage infrastructure, laboratories, to make and test prototypes/products, pilot plant production facilities, software development facilities, training and human resource development centres, propagate new scientific and technological development and innovation from idea stage to full-scale commercial production or any part of this chain.

metadata.topic: Sathak Innovation and Incubation Foundation (SIIF) - Main Objectives

text: Physical office space: approx. 5000 sq.ft.
Event halls: 100-seater, 400-seater, 700-seater halls for Entrepreneurship events.
Funding opportunities: grants, seed funding, and connections to potential investors.
Mentorship programs: experienced professionals and industry experts provide guidance.
Access to network of investors, venture capitalists, and angel investors.
Workshops, training programs, and events for innovation and entrepreneurial skills.
Partnerships with academic institutions, industry bodies, and government agencies.

To cater the needs of student entrepreneurs with innovative ideas thereby introducing a culture of entrepreneurship inside campus which will strengthen our education system and thereby promoting national economic and social growth.

metadata.topic: National Innovation and Startup Policy (NISP) - Vision

text: To develop an ecosystem with required infrastructure that can enable students and faculty to innovate and prototype their potential ideas with industrial standards and support from Government, industry and reputed academic institutions around the world and help them to realize their potentials.

metadata.topic: National Innovation and Startup Policy (NISP) - Mission

text: To help student groups to prototype their ideas.

metadata.topic: National Innovation and Startup Policy (NISP) - Short Term Objectives

text: To improve innovation, creative and design thinking among student community.

metadata.topic: National Innovation and Startup Policy (NISP) - Short Term Objectives

text: Incubation facility for faculty driven start-up and student/alumni start-up.

metadata.topic: National Innovation and Startup Policy (NISP) - Short Term Objectives

text: Organize FDP, seminars and workshops, distinguish talks for students, faculty and alumni and promote entrepreneurial culture.

metadata.topic: National Innovation and Startup Policy (NISP) - Short Term Objectives

text: Strengthen institute-industry interaction cell activity and effectively use the outcomes for achieving the mission.

metadata.topic: National Innovation and Startup Policy (NISP) - Short Term Objectives

text: Associate with DST, CII, MSME and other academic institutions for transferring world class facility for MSAJCE IEDC activities.

metadata.topic: National Innovation and Startup Policy (NISP) - Long Term Objectives

text: Improve quality of research work among students and to attain patent, which can be commercially used in production.

metadata.topic: National Innovation and Startup Policy (NISP) - Long Term Objectives

text: Provide a platform for students to develop innovative products with global recognition and generate business opportunities.

metadata.topic: National Innovation and Startup Policy (NISP) - Long Term Objectives

text: Generate revenues through consultancy work and student start-ups.

metadata.topic: National Innovation and Startup Policy (NISP) - Long Term Objectives

text: Spread awareness to students and faculty regarding IPR related activities.

metadata.topic: National Innovation and Startup Policy (NISP) - Long Term Objectives

text: Strategic partnership linkage with various organizations including Entrepreneurship Development Institute of India (EDII) and National Innovation Foundation (NIF).

metadata.topic: National Innovation and Startup Policy (NISP) - Long Term Objectives

text: Dr. K.S. Srinivasan — Principal, Mohamed Sathak A.J. College of Engineering (MSAJCE), Chennai - President.
Mr. Prakadesh Subramanian — Strategic Partner, SIIF, Chennai - Incubation Coordinator.
Mr. Parvez Aalam — CEO, Crescent Innovation Incubation Council, Chennai - Incubation Coordinator.
Mr. Thiyagaraja Gupta — Deputy Controller of Patents and Designs - Patent Expert.
Ahamed Jamel AMT — IT Consultant, 4A Technology - Alumni Entrepreneur.
Abdur Rahim Salih — Director & CEO, V5 Innovations Pvt. Ltd - Alumni Entrepreneur.
Asim Ali. L — CMO, Customer Labs - Alumni Entrepreneur.
To develop Strategies and governance for Promoting Innovation Entrepreneurship in the institute.
Creating Innovation Pipeline and Pathways for Entrepreneurs and Startups.
Building Organizational Capacity, Human Resources and Incentive.
Collaboration, Co-creation, Business Relationship and Knowledge Exchange.
Incubation & Pre-Incubation support at SIIF.
IP Ownership Rights for Technologies Developed at MSAJCE and SIIF.
Pedagogy & Learning Interventions for Supporting Innovations & Start-ups.
Entrepreneurial Performance Impact Assessment.
Increase in Self-Employment Rate; 5 Established Start-ups per year.
Establish Environment with multiple levels of support for innovation & Entrepreneurship and startups; Students will choose Entrepreneurship as career.
All students & faculty with entrepreneurship orientation; Students & faculty motivated to start entrepreneurial activity; 5 IPR/Innovations developed ideas/year.
5 Education/Skill certification programs on Entrepreneurship, IPR, Innovation; 25 workshops, awareness, market outreach events; 12 networking events (Intra and Inter-institutional) organized.
One Day Workshop on Entrepreneurship and Innovation as Career Opportunity - One per Year.
Business Plan Contest - One per Year.
Encourage students to become entrepreneurs by organizing entrepreneurship development programs and making them participate in activities organized by external agencies.

metadata.topic: Entrepreneurship Development Cell (EDC) - Roles and Responsibilities

text: Mr. S.V. Vinodh / EEE — Head EDC & HEC.
IIC-MSAJCE has been constituted as per guidelines of the Ministry of Human Resource Development Innovation Cell (MIC).
To be a centre of excellence for transforming students into proficient Electrical and Electronics Engineers through sustainable practices.
To create a vibrant local innovation ecosystem.
Identify and reward innovations and share success stories.

Lean Startup & Minimum Viable Product/Business - 23.08.2023; 110 Students, 05 Faculties - CSE Department.
